title: sanguisorba minor subsp. anceps
intro: Sanguisorba minor subsp. anceps, also known as red flowering burnet or dwarf burnet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high tannin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Sanguisorba minor subsp. anceps include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal infusions and poultices to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
